source_docs.title: Massively parallel digital transcriptional profiling of single cells
Supplementary Information for massively parallel digital transcriptional profiling

oligos.name: Beads-oligo-dT (gel-bead barcoded capture primer)
oligos.role: Gel-bead-tethered RT primer that captures polyadenylated mRNA and adds the P7 adapter, Read 2 primer site, 14 bp 10x cell barcode and 10 bp UMI

components.name: 10x Barcode
components.role: 14 bp cell barcode drawn from ~750,000 designed sequences

components.name: Poly(dT)VN
components.role: anchored 30 bp oligo-dT that primes poly(A) RNA

oligos.notes: Paper Fig.1d / protocol Step 2 describe the gel-bead oligo as, 5'->3': Illumina P7 + R2 sequence, 14 bp 10x barcode, 10 bp randomer (UMI), anchored 30 bp oligo-dT. Some early v1 libraries used a 5 bp UMI. The 'VN' anchor is implied by 'anchored 30 bp oligo-dT'; the exact IUPAC anchor bases are not printed in the documents.

oligos.name: Template Switching Oligo (TSO)
oligos.role: Template-switch oligo captured at the 3' end of first-strand cDNA to add a PCR handle for full-length amplification
oligos.notes: The paper explicitly states each cDNA 'ends with a template switching oligo at the 3' end' and that amplification uses 'primers complementary to the switch oligos'. The exact TSO sequence is not printed in these documents; shown here is the canonical 10x/SMART template-switch oligo (terminal rGrGrG folded to GGG).

oligos.name: cDNA Forward Primer (TSO PCR handle)
oligos.role: Forward PCR primer complementary to the template-switch oligo handle during cDNA amplification
oligos.notes: Provided in the kit as 'cDNA Primer Mix' (PN 220066); the forward primer is complementary to the switch-oligo handle. Sequence not printed in the documents; canonical 10x cDNA PCR handle shown.

oligos.name: cDNA Reverse Primer
oligos.role: Reverse PCR primer complementary to the P7/Read 2 sequencing adapter on the gel-bead strand during cDNA amplification
oligos.notes: Provided in the kit as 'cDNA Primer Mix' (PN 220066). Paper: amplification uses 'primers complementary to the switch oligos and sequencing adapters.' Sequence not printed; matches the P7 adapter carried on the bead oligo.

oligos.name: R1 Adaptor (Read 1 primer site)
oligos.role: Ligated adapter that installs the Read 1 sequencing primer site on the sheared cDNA end during library construction
oligos.notes: Kit 'R1 Adaptor Mix' (PN 220064), ligated after end-repair/A-tailing. Sequence not printed in the documents; shown is the partial TruSeq Read 1 primer site that is the Read 1 sequencing handle in the finished library.

oligos.name: SI-PCR Primer
oligos.role: Common primer in the Sample Index PCR that regenerates the P7 end of the library
oligos.notes: Kit 'SI-PCR Primer' (PN 220068), used together with an individual Sample Index. Exact sequence not printed in the documents; matches the constant P7 end.

oligos.name: Single Cell 3' Sample Index (i5)
oligos.role: Indexed PCR primer that adds the P5 adapter and the 8 bp sample index (incorporated as the i5 index read) during Sample Index PCR
oligos.notes: Single Cell 3' Sample Index Plate (PN 210136). Each named sample index combines 4 different sequences to base-balance across all 4 nucleotides. Individual index sequences are listed only in the sample-index set and are not printed in these documents.

oligos.name: Illumina TruSeq Read 1 primer
oligos.role: Standard Illumina Read 1 sequencing primer; reads the cDNA insert (98 nt)
oligos.notes: Protocol: 'Single Cell libraries use standard Illumina sequencing primers... require no custom primers.' Sequence is the standard TruSeq Read 1 primer.

oligos.name: Illumina TruSeq Read 2 primer
oligos.role: Standard Illumina Read 2 sequencing primer; reads the 10 bp UMI
oligos.notes: Standard TruSeq Read 2 primer; also present on the gel-bead oligo as the R2 site.

oligos.name: Illumina i7 Index sequencing primer
oligos.role: Standard Illumina i7 index-read primer; reads the 14 bp 10x cell barcode
oligos.notes: Standard i7 index sequencing primer; the 14 bp barcode is encoded at the start of the i7 index read.

oligos.name: Illumina P5 adapter
oligos.role: Flow-cell P5 bridge-amplification adapter (5' end of the finished library)

oligos.name: Illumina P7 adapter
oligos.role: Flow-cell P7 bridge-amplification adapter (3' end of the finished library)
oligos.notes: In the finished top strand the P7 end appears as its reverse complement, ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG.

final_library.source_label: 10x Chromium Single Cell 3' v1 finished Illumina library (paired-end, dual-index)
annotation_lines: CTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT = Partial TruSeq Read 1 (Read 1 primer site)

evidence.locator: 10x Chromium Single Cell 3' v1 finished Illumina library (paired-end, dual-index)

library_generation.title: GEM formation & mRNA capture on gel-bead oligo
library_generation.summary: Single cells and barcoded gel beads are co-partitioned into GEMs where the released oligo-dT primer anneals to poly(A) mRNA.
library_generation.note: Gel beads dissolve inside each GEM, releasing millions of copies of the barcoded capture primer (P7 + Read 2 + 14 bp barcode + 10 bp UMI + anchored oligo-dT). The oligo-dT hybridizes to the poly(A) tail of cell mRNA.

library_generation.title: Reverse transcription (GEM-RT)
library_generation.summary: Reverse transcriptase extends from the oligo-dT to synthesize barcoded first-strand cDNA.
library_generation.note: RT (55 C, 2 h) copies the mRNA into first-strand cDNA carrying the shared GEM barcode and UMI. Every cDNA from one GEM shares the same 14 bp barcode.

library_generation.title: Template switching
library_generation.summary: The RT adds untemplated nucleotides that the TSO templates, appending a PCR handle to the 3' end of the cDNA.
library_generation.note: Template switching installs a common forward PCR handle (from the TSO) at the cDNA 3' end for full-length amplification.

library_generation.title: cDNA amplification (bulk PCR)
library_generation.summary: Pooled barcoded cDNA is PCR-amplified with primers against the TSO handle and the P7/Read 2 adapter.
library_generation.note: The emulsion is broken, cDNA pooled and amplified (12-14 cycles) to generate double-stranded, full-length barcoded cDNA of sufficient mass for library construction.

library_generation.title: Fragmentation, end repair & A-tailing
library_generation.summary: Amplified cDNA is sheared to ~200 bp and the fragments are end-repaired and A-tailed.
library_generation.note: Covaris shearing to a ~200 bp peak; only fragments retaining the barcode/UMI end remain useful for the assay. Blunt ends are repaired and dA-tailed.

library_generation.title: R1 adaptor ligation
library_generation.summary: A T-A ligation installs the Read 1 sequencing primer site on the cDNA-insert end.
library_generation.note: The R1 Adaptor Mix ligates the partial TruSeq Read 1 handle to the A-tailed insert end, providing the Read 1 priming site.

library_generation.title: Sample Index PCR (finished library)
library_generation.summary: PCR with the SI-PCR primer and an indexed P5 primer adds P5 and the 8 bp i5 sample index and regenerates P7.
library_generation.note: Ten cycles add the P5 adapter and i5 sample index (from the Sample Index Plate) at the Read 1 end and regenerate P7 at the barcode end, producing the finished, Illumina-ready dual-index library.

library_sequencing.note: Read 1 primer anneals to the bottom strand at the partial Read 1 handle; its 3' end sits at the cDNA junction and reads 98 nt of the cDNA insert (shorter reads reduce alignment rate).
library_sequencing.diagram:
Read 1 primer (TruSeq R1)
     5'-ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT----------->   reads cDNA (98 nt)
top  5'-...........CTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT[====== cDNA insert ======](A)30...-3'
bot  3'-...........GATGTGCTGCGAGAAGGCTAGA[=== cDNA rev-complement ===](T)30...-5'
                                        (bottom strand = template)

library_sequencing.note: The i7 index read reads the 14 bp 10x cell barcode, which lies immediately 5' of the Read 2 (P7-proximal) constant handle on the top strand.
library_sequencing.diagram:
i7 index primer anneals at the R2/P7-proximal handle
top 5'-...(A)30 [UMI:10] NNNNNNNNNNNNNN AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC ATCTCG...-3'
                                    3'-TCTAGCCTTCTCGTGTGCAGACTTGAGGTCAGTG-5'
bot 3'-...(T)30 [UMIrc]  NNNNNNNNNNNNNN TCTAGCCTTCTCGTGTGCAGACTTGAGGTCAGTG TAGAGC...-5'
                       <------------- read = 14 bp cell barcode (i7)

library_sequencing.primer: Illumina i5 Index sequencing primer (P5-region: AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC)
library_sequencing.note: The i5 index read reads the 8 bp sample index adjacent to P5; each named sample index is a base-balanced mix of 4 sequences.
library_sequencing.diagram:
i5 index primer anneals to bottom strand at P5
        5'-AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC----->   reads 8 bp sample index
top 5'-AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC NNNNNNNN CTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT...-3'
bot 3'-TTACTATGCCGCTGGTGGCTCTAGATGTG NNNNNNNN GATGTGCTGCGAGAAGGCTAGA...-5'
                                    ------> read = 8 bp sample index (i5)

library_sequencing.note: Read 2 primer anneals to the top strand at the Read 2 handle (reverse complement) and reads the 10 bp UMI attached during reverse transcription; the 14 bp barcode is captured separately as the i7 index. Some early v1 libraries used a 5 bp UMI (5 bp Read 2).
library_sequencing.diagram:
Read 2 primer (TruSeq R2) anneals to top strand
top 5'-...(A)30 [UMI:10] [BC:14] AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC ATCTCG...-3'
                             3'-TCTAGCCTTCTCGTGTGCAGACTTGAGGTCAGTG-5'
bot 3'-...(T)30 [UMIrc]  [BCrc] TCTAGCCTTCTCGTGTGCAGACTTGAGGTCAGTG TAGAGC...-5'
            <--- read 2 = 10 bp UMI

title: Chromium Single Cell 3' v1
description: Droplet-based 3' single-cell RNA-seq (10x Genomics GemCode/Chromium). Single cells and barcoded gel beads are co-encapsulated in Gel Bead-in-EMulsion (GEM) partitions; each gel-bead oligo carries an Illumina P7 + Read 2 primer, a 14 bp 10x cell barcode, a 10 bp UMI randomer and an anchored 30 bp oligo-dT that primes reverse transcription of polyadenylated mRNA. Barcoded cDNA is pooled, amplified, sheared and finished into Illumina paired-end libraries. In this v1 chemistry, Read 1 sequences the cDNA insert, Read 2 reads the 10 bp UMI, the i7 index read reads the 14 bp cell barcode, and the i5 index read reads the 8 bp sample index — giving digital 3' transcript counts for thousands of single cells per channel.

reference.label: Massively parallel digital transcriptional profiling of single cells

original_publication.title: Massively parallel digital transcriptional profiling of single cells

throughput.summary: ~1,000-6,000 cells recovered per channel (default loading ~2,600 cells -> ~1,200 recovered, ~1.1% multiplet), across up to 8 channels per chip run in ~6 min; ~50% cell capture rate. ~250k single cells across 29 samples were profiled in the study.
throughput.cells: 1,000-23,000 cells loaded per channel; ~1,200-6,000 recovered; up to 8 channels/run
throughput.rna: ~1 pg RNA/cell (PBMCs) to ~15 pg RNA/cell (293T/3T3); median ~4,500 genes and ~27,000 UMIs per cell at 100k reads/cell
throughput.dna: cDNA sheared to ~200 bp for library construction

publication.statistical_model: Poisson loading of cells/gel beads into GEMs (multiplet rate consistent with Poisson statistics); genotype/chimerism inference uses a binomial mixture model over SNVs (two-genome mixture, K=1 vs K=2) fit with a Gibbs sampler.

other.label: Core technology
other.value: Gel Bead-in-EMulsion (GEM); 8-channel microfluidic chip, ~80% single-gel-bead fill rate

other.label: Sequencer validated
other.value: Illumina NextSeq 500 (2x75, V2) and HiSeq 2500 Rapid Run; loaded at 2.1 pM (NextSeq) / 10 pM (HiSeq)

other.label: Kit part numbers
other.value: GemCode Single-Cell 3' Gel Bead & Library Kit PN 120230/120231/120232; protocol CG00026 Rev B

other.label: Data availability
other.value: SRA accession SRP073767; analysis code at github.com/10XGenomics/single-cell-3prime-paper

data_processing.summary: The Cell Ranger Single-Cell Software Suite demultiplexes samples by the 8 bp index, extracts and error-corrects the 14 bp cell barcode and 10 bp UMI, aligns the 98 nt cDNA Read 1 with STAR, collapses PCR duplicates by (barcode, UMI, gene) to produce a gene-barcode matrix, then calls cells and clusters them (PCA -> k-means -> tSNE). Optional de-novo SNV calling (FreeBayes) with a binomial two-genome mixture model resolves genotype/chimerism.
data_processing.statistical_model: No parametric model for UMI expression counts (duplicates collapsed by 1-Hamming barcode/UMI correction); genotype/chimerism inferred with a binomial two-genome mixture model (K=1 vs K=2) fit by a Gibbs sampler; cell loading follows Poisson statistics.
